title: The Analects
author: Confucius
content: Do not do to others what you do not want done to yourself. This is the essence of the Golden Rule. When you go out, behave as if you were receiving an important guest. When employing the services of the common people, behave as if you were officiating at a great sacrifice. Do not do to others what you do not want them to do to you. In this way, you will be free from ill will whether at home or abroad. The strength of a nation derives from the integrity of the home. The strength of a home derives from the integrity of the individual. The strength of an individual derives from the integrity of the mind.
questions_raised: How does the Golden Rule apply to interactions with artificial beings?
What constitutes 'ill will' in the modern world?
Can integrity be maintained when systems conflict?
insights_gained: Reciprocal respect is fundamental to ethical systems
Personal integrity scales to societal strength
The Golden Rule requires empathy and self-reflection
